rationale: Subject home is a single-story ranch with a full hip roof (no gable ends, rake = 0). The plan is L-shaped: a main rectangular body (~280 px E-W × 205 px N-S = ~51 × 38 ft) plus a NE-projecting section (~185 px × 140 px = ~34 × 26 ft), likely an attached garage. Eave polygon traced at 8 vertices using the orange bounding box as reference (box spans ~347 × 275 px = 63.5 × 50.3 ft). Shoelace formula on the pixel polygon yields ~73,100 sq px × 0.033504 = 2,449 sq ft (~77% of bounding box, consistent with L-shape hip fill). Eaves summed around the full exterior perimeter (~222 ft). Ridges estimated from visible centerline peaks: ~15 ft main body + ~10 ft NE section. Hip lines estimated from 45° plan geometry: 4 corners on main body (~26.5 ft each) + 2 exposed corners on NE section (~18 ft each) = ~106 + 36 ft, rounded to 105 ft total. Valley where NE section meets main body estimated at ~26 ft. White vehicle visible at north end is parked in driveway and excluded. Confidence is moderate due to tree shadow and oblique view partially obscuring some eave corners.
